spec.name: PublicNews
spec.purpose: A minimal public GitHub repository that acts as a free GitHub Actions runner for private Telegram bot code. The bot code lives in a private repo; this repo only holds workflow YAML files, state JSON files, and a README. No bot source code is committed here.
spec.trigger: Schedule: every 20 minutes (cron "*/20 * * * *") → runs news_bot.py Schedule: 07:00 and 19:00 UTC daily (cron "0 7,19 * * *") → runs summary_bot.py Schedule: every 12 hours (cron "0 */12 * * *") → runs refresh_conflicts_cache.py Manual (workflow_dispatch) → all workflows support manual run
spec.private_repo: Source: github.com/alona-zubenko/telegram-war-news-bot (PRIVATE) Access: PAT stored as secret PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN (read-only scope: contents) Clone method: git clone with token embedded in URL (standard GitHub Actions pattern) Branch: main
spec.state_management: Strategy: Option A — state files live in PublicNews repo (not in private repo) Files: posted.json, war_articles.json Rationale: Simpler; state contains only URLs + article titles (not sensitive); avoids needing write PAT to private repo. After each run: workflow commits updated state files back to PublicNews/main using standard github-actions[bot] pattern with [skip ci] tag. Note: On first run, posted.json / war_articles.json are initialized as empty JSON files if they don't exist yet.
spec.inputs: PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN ← PAT with read access to alona-zubenko/telegram-war-news-bot BOT_TOKEN ← Telegram bot token CHANNEL_ID ← Telegram channel numeric ID NEWSAPI_KEY ← NewsAPI key GROQ_API_KEY ← Groq API key CHANNEL_USERNAME ← e.g. WarConflictsUA EXPRESS_CHANNEL_ID ← Express channel numeric ID EXPRESS_CHANNEL_USERNAME ← Express channel username
spec.logic_steps: Step 1: Checkout PublicNews repo (to get state files: posted.json, war_articles.json) Step 2: Set up Python 3.12 Step 3: Clone private repo into subdirectory ./bot/ → git clone https://${{ secrets.PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN }}@github.com/alona-zubenko/telegram-war-news-bot.git bot Step 4: Install dependencies from ./bot/requirements.txt Step 5: Copy state files from workspace root into ./bot/ (so bot reads them) → cp posted.json war_articles.json ./bot/ (if they exist) Step 6: Run bot script from ./bot/ directory with all env vars injected Step 7: Copy updated state files back from ./bot/ to workspace root Step 8: Commit state files to PublicNews repo with [skip ci] tag (incremental commit, never force-push — TG_github_cron_disabled_after_force_push)
spec.files_in_repo: .github/workflows/news_bot.yml .github/workflows/war_summary.yml .github/workflows/refresh_conflicts_cache.yml .github/workflows/delete_all.yml posted.json ← state file, committed by workflows war_articles.json ← state file, committed by workflows README.md ← explains repo purpose (no bot code here)
spec.private_repo_changes: Disable all 4 workflows in alona-zubenko/telegram-war-news-bot: → GitHub UI: Actions → each workflow → "Disable workflow" OR: add [skip ci] or rename workflow files to *.yml.disabled Bot code and files remain untouched in private repo.
spec.edge_cases: 1. State files missing on first run → workflows initialize empty JSON before copy step 2. Private repo clone fails (bad PAT / expired token) → workflow fails fast with clear error 3. Cron disabled after force-push → NEVER force-push; always incremental commit (TG_github_cron_disabled_after_force_push) 4. Bot script writes state to ./bot/ but state must persist in PublicNews → explicit copy-back step 5. delete_all.py resets state → after run, commit empty posted.json + war_articles.json 6. refresh_conflicts_cache.py may not exist in private repo → check before calling, skip gracefully
spec.out_of_scope: - Modifying news_bot.py or any bot source code - Running EcoNewsBot from this repo (separate decision needed) - Storing secrets in code or README - Any bot logic changes
spec.success_criteria: Given news_bot workflow triggers at */20 → PASS: news_bot.py runs, posts to Telegram, posted.json updated and committed to PublicNews Given private repo PAT expires → PASS: workflow fails at clone step with clear "authentication failed" error, not silently Given force-push is attempted → PASS: workflow uses incremental commit; cron schedule remains active (no re-registration needed) Given first run (empty state files) → PASS: bot initializes empty state, no crash Given delete_all runs manually → PASS: channel cleared; posted.json and war_articles.json reset to empty in PublicNews
